title: Types of data, methods and research design
paperName: Paper 1: Socialisation, identity and methods of research
summary: 9699 — primary/secondary data, quantitative/qualitative methods, and research design choices.
learningObjectives: Distinguish primary and secondary data sources.
Explain sampling techniques and their limitations.
Design appropriate research for a given hypothesis.

simpleExplanation.title: How sociologists find out
simpleExplanation.summary: Research design matches the question: surveys and statistics for patterns; interviews and observation for depth and meaning.

flashcards.back: Collected firsthand by researcher for their study.
Already exists — official stats, previous studies, documents.
Structured, numerical — surveys, experiments, content analysis.
Rich, detailed — unstructured interviews, PO, focus groups.
Each member of population has equal chance — reduces bias if large enough.
Hard-to-reach groups — participants refer others; risk of unrepresentative sample.

sections.content: **Research methods** (2.1) is core Paper 1 skills. Always state **strengths and limitations** of each method. Methods questions often require **matching method to aim**.

sections.question: Design a study to investigate why some students skip school. State aim, method, sample, and two limitations. [12 marks]
sections.solution:
**Aim:** Explore meanings and pressures behind truancy (interpretivist emphasis).

**Method:** **Semi-structured interviews** with 20 truant students + **anonymous questionnaire** to 200 pupils for patterns.

**Sample:** **Stratified** by year group and gender from one secondary school; **snowball** for persistent truants hard to reach.

**Strengths:** Interviews give **depth** (fear, bullying, boredom); survey shows **prevalence**.

**Limitations:** (1) **Social desirability** — students may under-report; (2) **School-specific** — not generalisable nationally; (3) **Researcher effect** in interviews.

**Ethical note:** Parental consent, anonymity, safeguarding if abuse disclosed.
